label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, Jane booked a flight for tomorrow, which is July 29, 2002. The question is asking for the date one week ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. Let me break this down step by step to make sure I don't make any mistakes. First, let's clarify the dates. If the flight is booked for tomorrow, that means "today" is the day before the flight. So if the flight is on July 29, 2002, then today must be July 28, 2002. Now, the question is asking for the date one week ago from today. So, one week before July 28, 2002. To find a date one week ago, we need to subtract 7 days from July 28, 2002. Let me count backward day by day. Starting from July 28, subtracting 1 day is July 27. Subtracting 2 days is July 26. Three days ago is July 25. Four days is July 24. Five days is July 23. Six days is July 22. Seven days is July 21. So, subtracting 7 days from July 28 lands us on July 21, 2002. Now, let me check the options. Option E is 07/21/2002. That seems to match. But wait, let me double-check in case there's a mistake. Alternatively, maybe I should verify by adding a week to July 21 to see if it gets to July 28. July has 31 days, so July 21 plus 7 days is July 28. Yes, that's correct. So, July 21 is indeed a week before July 28. But hold on, the question says "one week ago from today." Since today is July 28, then yes, subtracting a week is July 21. Therefore, the correct answer should be July 21, 2002, which is option E. Wait, let me check the options again to make sure I didn't mix up the numbers. The options are: (A) 08/18/2002 – August 18, which is later in the year, so not correct. (B) 10/21/2002 – October 21, too far. (C) 07/20/2002 – July 20, which is only 8 days before July 28 (July 20 to 28 is 8 days), so that's 8 days ago, not 7. (D) 10/17/2002 – October 17, way off. (E) 07/21/2002 – July 21, as we calculated. Correct. (F) 11/21/2001 – November 21 of the previous year, that's over 8 months earlier. Not correct. So option E is the right answer here. I think that's solid. Let me just make sure there's no trick in the question. The flight is for tomorrow (Jul 29), so today is Jul 28. One week before today is Jul 21. Yep, that's straightforward. No leap
